body.title: cortex.delete_memory doesn't cascade to memory_embeddings/blobs — NE merge loops + floods logs at MB/sec
body.result: cortex.delete_memory (cortex.py:3880) now deletes memory_embeddings + memory_blobs rows first, then memories — all in one _conn context. 3 tests pass (without-embedding, nonexistent, source-regression); 1 skipped (pgvector insert schema). Igor needs restart to pick up. NE merge loop will then succeed and stop flooding error pipeline.
body.description:
cortex.delete_memory (cortex.py:3880) runs a plain DELETE on the memories table. memory_embeddings.memory_id and memory_blobs.memory_id both hold FOREIGN KEY constraints referencing memories.id. Any memory with an embedding (all of them, post T-db-populate-embeddings) fails to delete with: `violates foreign key constraint memory_embeddings_memory_id_fkey`.

NE (narrative_engine._merge_cluster, narrative_engine.py:1836) loops over a cluster calling delete_memory on each; each failure writes to cc_alerts.log + errors.log + master.log + cognition.log. Observed 2026-04-24 PM: disk write rate spiked >200MB/sec sustained while Igor churned through merge attempts. Akien stopped Igor to contain.

Fix: delete_memory must FK-aware-cascade within a single transaction — DELETE from memory_embeddings and memory_blobs keyed on memory_id first, then DELETE from memories. Pattern established in test_data_lifecycle.py and the one-shot dupe cleanup (T-duplicate-memory-cleanup).

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py:3880 (delete_memory), tests/test_cortex_delete_memory.py (new, or add to existing cortex tests).
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** IN — FK cascade in delete_memory. OUT — redesigning NE merge, adding cascade to DB schema migrations (that's separate), rate-limiting NE log fanout.
**Test plan:** deposit a memory + an embedding, call delete_memory, assert both rows gone + returns True. Delete of nonexistent memory returns False. Transaction rollback on failure preserves both tables.
